Code fragment: public final TableField<ItemRecord, Long> ID = createField("ID", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).defaulted(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is named just 'ID', which is a generic name that doesn't convey any meaning about the table it belongs to. Consider renaming it to something more descriptive like 'ITEM_ID' to make joins and queries more readable and self-documenting.

Code fragment: public final TableField<EmployeeDepartmentRecord, Long> ID = createField("ID", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).defaulted(true), this, "");
Explanation: This junction table uses a synthetic auto-generated 'ID' primary key when the natural composite key of (EMPLOYEE_ID, DEPARTMENT_ID) would be more appropriate. Consider using a composite primary key on the two foreign key columns instead, which would also enforce uniqueness of the employee-department relationship.

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the DEPARTMENT table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Specify only the required columns explicitly, e.g., context.select(Tables.DEPARTMENT.ID, Tables.DEPARTMENT.NAME).from(Tables.DEPARTMENT).

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from all joined tables (DEPARTMENT, EMPLOYEE_DEPARTMENT, EMPLOYEE), which retrieves unnecessary data. Explicitly list only the columns needed for EmployeeRecord and DepartmentRecord.

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from all joined tables, potentially retrieving unnecessary data. Explicitly specify only the columns required for populating EmployeeRecord and DepartmentRecord.
